title: Secure-SDLC security requirements
description: Define, document, and maintain security requirements for both the software development infrastructure and the software being built, and communicate third-party component requirements. Implements SSDF PO.1.

tasks.title: Document infrastructure & software security requirements
tasks.description: Capture security requirements for the development infrastructure and for the software, and store them where the team can reference them.

tasks.title: Track third-party component requirements
tasks.description: Record provenance and security expectations for reused commercial/open-source components.

tasks.title: Review requirements at least annually
tasks.description: Revisit and update the requirements as regulations, threats, and the tech stack change.

title: Secure development roles, responsibilities, and training
description: Assign SDLC security roles, provide role-based secure-development training, and obtain leadership commitment to secure development. Implements SSDF PO.2.

tasks.title: Define SDLC security roles
tasks.description: Document who is responsible for each security-relevant activity across the SDLC.

tasks.title: Deliver role-based secure-development training
tasks.description: Train developers, reviewers, and operators on the practices relevant to their role; refresh periodically.

tasks.title: Secure leadership commitment
tasks.description: Obtain and communicate management sponsorship for the secure-development program.

title: Security toolchain (SAST / DAST / SCA)
description: Specify, integrate, and securely operate the security tooling in the toolchain, and configure it to produce evidence artifacts. Implements SSDF PO.3.

tasks.title: Select mandatory security tools
tasks.description: Decide which SAST, DAST, SCA, and secrets-scanning tools are required and how they integrate into CI.

tasks.title: Operate the toolchain securely
tasks.description: Deploy, patch, and access-control the tooling following recommended practices.

tasks.title: Configure evidence artifact generation
tasks.description: Ensure tools emit machine-readable results that can be retained as compliance evidence.

title: Software security check criteria and release gates
description: Define the criteria that software must meet before release and gather the information needed to make informed go/no-go decisions. Implements SSDF PO.4.

tasks.title: Define security check / release gate criteria
tasks.description: Set the pass/fail criteria (e.g. no unresolved criticals) for each stage of the SDLC.

tasks.title: Gather and safeguard decision evidence
tasks.description: Collect scan results, review sign-offs, and test evidence and provide them to release approvers.

title: Hardened, separated development environments
description: Separate and protect each development environment and harden development, build, and test endpoints using a risk-based approach. Implements SSDF PO.5.

tasks.title: Separate development, build, and production environments
tasks.description: Isolate environments with distinct credentials and network boundaries to limit blast radius.

tasks.title: Harden development endpoints
tasks.description: Apply baseline hardening, endpoint protection, and least-privilege access to developer and build systems.

title: Source-code integrity and access control
description: Store all forms of code under least privilege with branch protection and signed commits to prevent unauthorized access and tampering. Implements SSDF PS.1.

tasks.title: Enforce least-privilege repository access
tasks.description: Grant repository and pipeline access only to those who need it; review membership regularly.

tasks.title: Enable branch protection and signed commits
tasks.description: Require protected branches, mandatory reviews, and commit signing to prevent tampering.

title: Release-artifact signing and integrity verification
description: Provide cryptographic integrity-verification information (hashes, digital signatures) for each software release so acquirers can verify authenticity. Implements SSDF PS.2.

tasks.title: Sign release artifacts
tasks.description: Digitally sign build artifacts and publish the signing certificate/public key.

tasks.title: Publish integrity verification information
tasks.description: Make hashes and signatures available to acquirers alongside each release.

title: Release provenance and archival (SBOM)
description: Securely archive each release and its supporting data, and collect and share provenance data such as a software bill of materials. Implements SSDF PS.3.

tasks.title: Archive each release with supporting data
tasks.description: Retain release files, integrity information, and provenance for the required retention period.

tasks.title: Generate and maintain an SBOM
tasks.description: Produce a software bill of materials per release and keep it available for vulnerability response.

title: Threat modeling and secure design
description: Use threat modeling and attack-surface analysis to inform design, prefer standardized security features, and record security-relevant design decisions. Implements SSDF PW.1.

tasks.title: Threat model significant features
tasks.description: Run threat modeling or attack-surface mapping for new services and material design changes.

tasks.title: Prefer standardized security services
tasks.description: Reuse vetted auth, logging, and crypto services instead of building proprietary equivalents.

tasks.title: Record design decisions and risk responses
tasks.description: Document the security-relevant design choices and how identified risks are addressed.

title: Independent secure design review
description: Have a qualified reviewer or automated process independently confirm the design meets security requirements and addresses identified risks. Implements SSDF PW.2.

tasks.title: Conduct independent design review
tasks.description: Have someone not involved in the design (or tooling) verify it satisfies the security requirements before build.

title: Approved-component allowlist and SCA gate
description: Acquire and maintain well-secured components, reuse vetted internal modules, and verify all third-party components against requirements throughout their life. Implements SSDF PW.4.

tasks.title: Maintain an approved-component allowlist
tasks.description: Curate the components teams may use and the source they must come from.

tasks.title: Gate builds on software composition analysis
tasks.description: Run SCA in CI and fail builds that introduce disallowed or vulnerable dependencies.

tasks.title: Continuously verify acquired components
tasks.description: Re-check third-party components against requirements over their lifecycle, not just at adoption.

title: Secure coding standards
description: Adopt and follow secure coding practices appropriate to the languages and environments in use. Implements SSDF PW.5.

tasks.title: Publish language-appropriate secure coding standards
tasks.description: Document secure coding guidance for each primary language/framework used.

tasks.title: Enforce standards via linters and review
tasks.description: Wire the standards into linters and the code-review checklist so they are applied consistently.

title: Build hardening flags
description: Use compiler, interpreter, and build-tool features that improve executable security, and configure the approved hardening settings. Implements SSDF PW.6.

tasks.title: Select build tools with hardening features
tasks.description: Prefer toolchains offering stack protection, ASLR, fortified functions, and similar mitigations.

tasks.title: Enable and verify hardening flags
tasks.description: Turn on the approved hardening settings and verify builds actually carry them.

title: Mandatory peer code review
description: Determine where code review and/or analysis apply, then perform them against the secure coding standard and triage findings. Implements SSDF PW.7.

tasks.title: Require peer review before merge
tasks.description: Mandate at least one qualified reviewer per change on protected branches.

tasks.title: Triage review and analysis findings
tasks.description: Record and prioritize issues from review/analysis in the team's issue tracker and drive them to closure.

title: SAST and DAST in CI
description: Determine and perform executable-code testing (SAST/DAST, fuzzing, pen testing) and triage the results. Implements SSDF PW.8.

tasks.title: Run SAST and DAST in the pipeline
tasks.description: Integrate static and dynamic analysis into CI with defined failure thresholds.

tasks.title: Scope and triage test results
tasks.description: Design the tests, run them, and record/triage discovered issues with recommended remediations.

title: Secure-by-default configuration
description: Define a secure configuration baseline and ship the software with those defaults, documented for administrators. Implements SSDF PW.9.

tasks.title: Define the secure default baseline
tasks.description: Determine the secure value for every security-relevant setting.

tasks.title: Ship and document secure defaults
tasks.description: Implement the defaults and document each setting for administrators.

title: Vulnerability monitoring and disclosure policy
description: Continuously gather vulnerability information, confirm vulnerabilities, and maintain a vulnerability-disclosure and remediation policy. Implements SSDF RV.1.

tasks.title: Monitor internal and external vulnerability sources
tasks.description: Track advisories, SCA alerts, and reports for the software and its third-party components.

tasks.title: Maintain a coordinated disclosure policy
tasks.description: Publish how to report vulnerabilities and define the roles and process to handle them.

title: Vulnerability response and patch SLA
description: Assess and prioritize each vulnerability by risk and plan and implement remediations within defined SLAs. Implements SSDF RV.2.

tasks.title: Assess and prioritize vulnerabilities
tasks.description: Analyze each vulnerability's risk to plan its remediation or other response.

tasks.title: Remediate within SLA and notify
tasks.description: Patch or mitigate within severity-based SLAs and notify affected parties per the disclosure process.

title: Root-cause analysis and SDLC improvement
description: Analyze vulnerability root causes, look for patterns and similar defects, and update the SDLC to prevent recurrence. Implements SSDF RV.3.

tasks.title: Perform root-cause analysis
tasks.description: Determine the underlying cause of each significant vulnerability and look for the same class elsewhere.

tasks.title: Feed lessons back into the SDLC
tasks.description: Update secure-development practices and processes to prevent the root cause from recurring.
